channel.title: Strategy Publisher
channel.description: Strategy briefing for founders, consultants, and C-suite operators.

item.title: Agents Need a Memory Architecture
item.description: Research synthesis: production agents store experience in prompt rules and model weights that drift apart. Field analysis of 1,990 public discussions yields a three-episode memory architecture process.

item.title: The AI Disclosure Clock
item.description: News digest: EU AI Act Article 50 transparency duties apply 2 August 2026; Commission guidelines of 20 July make disclosure a two-week operator deadline.

item.title: The AI Coverage Gap
item.description: Generative AI insurance exclusions turn residual risk into an operator ledger: exposure class, policy line, exclusion surface, residual owner, control evidence, and stop rule.

item.title: The AI Browser Boundary
item.description: Agentic browsers turn a reading surface into an execution surface: session privilege, instruction channels, tool reach, data boundaries, work classes, and incident paths.

item.title: The AI Source Rights Ledger
item.description: AI search is unbundling the old crawler bargain into access, citation, answer, training, payment, and relationship rights.

item.title: The AI Control Gap Ledger
item.description: Enterprise AI accountability is outrunning visibility, action authority, financial exposure, incident response, and business ownership.

item.title: The AI Audience Ledger
item.description: AI search turns founder publishing into an audience ledger: crawl access, answer presence, referral quality, owned relationships, conversion rights, and proof loops.

item.title: The AI Slop Audit
item.description: AI-assisted work should be audited for accuracy, specificity, judgment, review burden, and learning before generated volume is mistaken for operating leverage.

item.title: The AI Memory Ledger
item.description: AI memory should be governed through a ledger of preference, role, project, sensitive, and institutional context before persistent personalization becomes hidden policy.

item.title: The AI Model Mix Ledger
item.description: AI buying should be managed through a ledger of frontier APIs, open-weight models, hosted models, vendor bundles, specialist models, and no-model boundaries.

item.title: The AI Productivity Gap
item.description: AI productivity should be judged through a ledger of adoption, daily use, workflow redesign, measurement, learning, and capital discipline.

item.title: The AI Distribution Boundary
item.description: AI search, workplace agents, and browser agents should be managed as a distribution boundary: discovery, evidence, action, context, relationship, conversion, and feedback.

item.title: The Agent Identity Boundary
item.description: AI agents should be governed through an identity boundary: actor, authority source, session, tool scope, human checkpoint, record, and revocation.

item.title: The AI Disclosure Ledger
item.description: AI transparency should be managed as a disclosure ledger: surface, audience, origin, control, and evidence.

item.title: The AI Evaluation Gap
item.description: AI evaluation should become a live evidence loop: task fit, workflow fit, failure map, cost burden, and learning cadence.

item.title: The AI Agent Permission Ledger
item.description: Agentic AI should be governed through a permission ledger: read, reason, write, spend, message, change, owner, audit trail, and stop rule.

item.title: The AI Work Cadence
item.description: AI adoption should be governed as a work cadence: artifact, rhythm, handoff, review burden, and job redesign.

item.title: The AI Token Budget
item.description: Inference should be governed as a token budget: model tier, context budget, agent loop, value class, and owner.

item.title: The AI Productivity Receipt
item.description: AI benchmark gains should become business commitments only after the company can show task, workflow, economic, and trust receipts.

item.title: The Founder Distribution Ledger
item.description: Founder publishing should be managed as a distribution ledger: public shelf, relationship rail, conversion right, and proof loop.

item.title: The ASEAN Market-Entry Test
item.description: ASEAN digital integration should be treated as a market-entry test: first buyer, operating reuse, trust bridge, regulatory drag, and regional option value.

item.title: The Stablecoin Distribution Test
item.description: Stablecoin payments should be judged as a distribution test: buyer access, settlement value, product fit, and operating control.

item.title: The AI Evidence Packet
item.description: AI procurement should move from demo judgment to evidence judgment before agents, copilots, and automation layers enter production workflows.

item.title: The AI Payback Ledger
item.description: AI capex should be judged by asset, obligation, payback owner, proof milestone, and stop rule before spending scales.

item.title: The AI Herding Problem
item.description: When AI makes analysis cheap and crowded, executives need a clear doctrine for which strategic decisions remain human-owned.

item.title: The AI Quality Test
item.description: AI spending should be judged as quality capital, a learning option, or theme exposure before funding expands.

item.title: The AI Data-Center Veto
item.description: Power, permitting, water, ratepayer politics, and capital discipline are becoming board-level constraints on AI strategy.

item.title: The AI Strategy Theatre Test
item.description: A five-part executive screen for separating strategic AI workflow change from demos, activity metrics, and unmanaged theatre.

item.title: The AI Management Layer Is Arriving
item.description: Enterprise AI is being folded into managed work, agent registries, observability logs, provenance rules, licensing, and transparency obligations.

item.title: The AI Vendor's Warning Label
item.description: Agent-security guidance is becoming the AI industry's shared-responsibility model: a practical safety manual today and a future control record after the first serious incident.

item.title: The AI Pilot Trap
item.description: Companies are not short of AI use cases. They are short of managerial judgment about where autonomy should enter the business and where it should stop.

item.title: AI Strategy Starts With Doctrine Before Tools
item.description: Founders do not have an AI tooling problem first. They have a doctrine problem.
